tossups.question: Per Craig Wright, a piece in this genre is based on the ratio 6 to 4 to 2 to 3 in reference to Solomon's Temple. The structure of another piece in this genre may be based on the gematria for its composer's surname, which nicely sums to 69. While writing The Magic Flute, Mozart composed a 46-bar long piece of this type for Anton Stoll. The consecration of the Florence Cathedral occasioned the writing of a piece in this genre called Nuper rosarum flores by Guillame Dufay (gee-YOME doo-FYE). This genre's English counterpart was the anthem. This is the genre of a 1570 piece for a horseshoe-shaped arrangement of eight choirs of five voices that sing "I have never put my hope in any other" in Latin. For 10 points, Mozart's Ave Verum Corpus and Thomas Tallis's Spem in Alium exemplify what polyphonic sacred vocal genre contrasted with the usually secular madrigal?
tossups.answer: motets [the first line refers to Nuper rosarum flores; the second to Spem in alium]
tossups.answer_sanitized: motets [the first line refers to Nuper rosarum flores; the second to Spem in alium]

The consecration of the Florence Cathedral occasioned the writing of a piece in this genre called Nuper rosarum flores by Guillame Dufay (gee-YOME doo-FYE). This genre's English counterpart was the anthem. For 10 points, Mozart's Ave Verum Corpus and Thomas Tallis's Spem in Alium exemplify what polyphonic sacred vocal genre contrasted with the usually secular madrigal?

tossups.question: This work formed the basis of a sound installation that Janet Cardiff exhibited in the Fuentidueña Chapel. It’s not by George Crumb, Istvan Marta, Shostakovich, or Ives, but the Kronos Quartet recorded this work for their album Black Angels. The length of this work may be a play on the total value, in gematria, of the name “Judith” and the name of this work’s composer, both of which equal 69. The Egerton and Gresham manuscripts are the principal sources of this work, whose structure may have been inspired by the architecture of Nonsuch Palace. Peter Gregson used multitracking to use a single cello to perform this work, which was likely commissioned by the Duke of Norfolk to rival Alessandro Striggio’s Ecce Beatam Lucem, which had a similar scale. This piece passes its melody in a horseshoe shape around eight five-voice choirs. For 10 points, name this 40-part motet by Thomas Tallis.
tossups.answer: Spem in Alium [or Spem in alium nunquam habui]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Spem in Alium [or Spem in alium nunquam habui]

bonuses.leadin: This piece is featured in a permanent exhibit at the National Gallery of Canada where each speaker in the Rideau Street Chapel plays a different voice. For 10 points each:
parts: Spem in alium was written by this English composer, who was the subject of a Fantasia by Ralph Vaughan Williams.
The Tallis theme used in Vaughan Williams’ Fantasia is in the Phrygian example of this musical feature. The different examples of this musical feature are all named for different Greek regions.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This piece is featured in a permanent exhibit at the National Gallery of Canada where each speaker in the Rideau Street Chapel plays a different voice. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Spem in alium was written by this English composer, who was the subject of a Fantasia by Ralph Vaughan Williams.
The Tallis theme used in Vaughan Williams' Fantasia is in the Phrygian example of this musical feature. The different examples of this musical feature are all named for different Greek regions.

bonuses.leadin: This work was commissioned after the performance of a similar piece by Alessandro Striggio, and it may have been written for the queen of England’s fortieth birthday. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this piece of music, a motet scored for eight choirs of fives voices each, that takes its text from a passage in the Book of Judith.
Spem in Alium was composed by this contemporary of William Byrd who wrote a ton of religious music. A notable “Fantasia on a Theme by” him was composed by Ralph Vaughan Williams.

This contemporary artist’s Forty Part Motet consists of playing the forty parts of Spem in Alium from different locations and allowing people to walk through the exhibit. Her works with George Bures Miller include The Murder of Crows and The Killing Machine.
answers_sanitized: Spem in Alium [or Hope in Any Other]

This contemporary artist's Forty Part Motet consists of playing the forty parts of Spem in Alium from different locations and allowing people to walk through the exhibit. Her works with George Bures Miller include The Murder of Crows and The Killing Machine.
